Get a head start on the Earth Day Celebration which is April 22 this year. **Solarize NoVA Launches April 15** _Since 2014 the campaign has resulted in more than 9 MW of new solar in region; $28+ Million in private sector investment_ The Northern Virginia Regional Commission, its local government partners and the Local Energy Alliance Program (LEAP) are pleased to announce this year’s Solarize NoVA campaign will kick off April 15. Since its inception in 2014, Solarize has been committed to supporting homeowners by offering free solar satellite assessments and education on the benefits of solar energy. The program has been widely successful, helping Northern Virginians save money and reduce their reliance on traditional energy sources. With the 2023 passing of the Inflation Reduction Act, the federal solar tax credit has increased from 26% to 30%, offering even more financial incentive for homeowners to make the switch to solar. As the nation navigates uncertainty, this may not always be the case. Homeowners and businesses interested in joining Solarize can sign up for a free solar satellite assessment and access discounted rates until July 15th. LEAP offers ongoing support throughout the process to ensure a seamless experience, from the initial consultation to installation. For more information on how to sign up for Solarize or to attend one of the free, informative webinars, please visit [https://www.solarizenova.org/](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106025766/540176833/-1476207771?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMTIwODA2ZDctY2IxYi1mMDExLThiM2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=_bsdPW8QmpIuN0Td2tAU_xyDVIV_DEx7WEgzdLTpxx0=&emci=1d2324bd-330b-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&emdi=120806d7-cb1b-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=22486556) **ArtsFairfax: Calling All Local Artists** ArtsFairfax posts opportunities to apply for new commissions, various art performances, or exhibition opportunities from across the country, throughout the region, or from within the County. The Arc of Northern Virginia Benefit Breakfast is one of my favorite events each year - stories shared remind us of what we can accomplish with the combination of government funding and private donations, a clear purpose and need, and compassion. People with disabilities want “A Life Like Yours.” The Arc of Northern Virginia represents and serves more than 39,000 individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities (ID/DD) and their families with direct service programs, education, and advocacy. However, the struggle for quality of life for these thousands of individuals and their families is daunting. Virginia remains 39th among the states in providing community supports for citizens with ID/DD and services are limited for those who need costly lifelong support to continue to live in their communities. Even with the recent Medicaid expansion, more than 15,000 people remain on waiting lists for critical services. They need our help as we continue to work to ensure their full inclusion and participation in our communities. clean_body: | | | Boysko Bulletin:Post Reconvene Catch Up Dear Peter, Martin Luther King wrote, “And one of the great problems of history is that the concepts of love and power have usually been contrasted as opposites—polar opposites, so that love is identified with a resignation of power and power with a denial of love. Power at its best is love implementing the demands of justice, and justice at its best is power correcting everything that stands against love.” Those words ring true today just as they did in the 1960’s. The Trump Administration has chosen to use power as a lever to invoke fear and anxiety. Stripping people of their jobs, eliminating important and life-saving programs, invoking fear into individuals for using their First Amendment rights. The time is now to stand together to reject these actions to use our voices to say Not On My Watch. I’ve been heartened that people all over Virginia and across the nation have begun to mobilize and organize to help federal workers who’ve recently lost their jobs, to stand up for students and our colleges and universities being stripped of funding or worse, those who are being deported for using their voices to oppose human rights atrocities. We have begun to see an outcry about the harm that comes with the end of federal grants to fund important health care research, safe drinking water, substance abuse and addiction recovery services, and for our public health departments. Every day I hear from my constituents who are being directly affected. My responsibility is to speak truth to power and as Dr. King stated so eloquently, to use the power and influence given to me by my community as he envisioned. “ Power at its best is love implementing the demands of justice, and justice at its best is power correcting everything that stands against love.” The time is now to be brave and clear about our values. I value freedom of speech; I value a robust public education; I value access to quality healthcare; I value the right make decisions about when and with whom a person chooses to marry or start a family; I believe that diversity and equity makes us stronger; And I believe that we gravely harm the economic standing of our nation when we strip individuals of the services and basic support systems that we have had in place for decades and create extraordinary tariffs on our allies around the world. We await the Governor’s signature on the bills with his amendments that the General Assembly rejected at the Reconvene Session, including the budget bill. The Governor has until May 2 at 11:59 pm to sign or veto . Many of the bills, particularly the budget bill, impact our daily life and it is frustrating that votes are partisan. The shows the difference between the General Assembly budget and the Governor’s budget amendments. Some major issues stand out. The governor proposes to reduce money that the legislature provided to remove the arbitrary cap on state funding for support staff - one of my budget amendments. Support staff keep schools clean, keep technology running, and much more. In lieu of supporting our public schools, the Governor’s amendments would fund private school vouchers and lab schools. The governor’s amendments also include eliminating funding to increase the minimum wage. At a time when the divide between the wealthiest few and those with the least continues to grow, his action dismisses an opportunity to protect Virginia’s workers and ensure a more fair wage for hundreds of thousands of working people across Virginia. The governor’s budget choices also include efforts to harm our immigrant neighbors and our communities by coercing local jails to enforce federal immigration detainers or face state funding cuts. This harms immigrant families and would be costly for localities by making facilities hold people up to two days after they would otherwise be released. The General Assembly was not able to override the Governor’s vetoes of legislation which requires a two-thirds vote. Six of my bills were vetoed. There are bills awaiting further action by the Governor following our rejection of his amendments. Despite overwhelming public support for contraception, nearly all Republicans voted against bills to protect women’s right to contraception, the Contraceptive Equity Act (HB2371 & ) and Right to Contraception Act (HB1716 & ) and the Governor’s amendments would have gutted the bills. I will continue to fight to keep Virginia safe for women and families. My work in Richmond is supported by passionate, intelligent advocates who are experts in their fields. Teamwork is critical to legislative success, as is listening to and respecting constituents. I am honored to be recognized by several groups for my work. I’ve been named Legislator of the Year and a 2025 Affordability Champion by Freedom Virginia. This year, we again fought to establish a Paid Family and Medical Leave program in Virginia. No one should have to choose between caring for themselves or a family member and their financial security. I have also been named . I have worked alongside ReproRising, formerly known as NARAL Virginia, for decades. I carried legislation fighting for women’s reproductive freedom when serving in the minority party. With control of the House and the Senate, I passed the constitutional amendment for reproductive freedom. The amendment must pass in a second post election session and then the voters of Virginia will have the opportunity to amend the constitution by passing a referendum. It is critical that Virginia remain safe for women and families to make their own decisions about when, if, and with whom they wish to have children. I’m also grateful for the support of the No Fairfax Casino advocacy group who spoke up early, did their research, and showed up for town halls in the district and in Richmond to reject a casino in their community. We’ve been successful for two years. The battle is likely not over but we will keep fighting because we do not believe that a casino is a good fit for the Tysons area. I appreciate their support at my recent town halls in McLean and in Reston. Often as we approach the next election, I remind everyone to vote and that their voice matters. This has never been clearer given the actions of the federal administration and the Governor's decision to veto and amend bills that would have helped working families. Hardly a day passes without new actions taken by the federal administration to dismantle our federal government. I have included information for those impacted by their actions below. Our November election is critical as the power to provide support to our most vulnerable Virginians, to provide funding for basics like maintenance of our roads, for our schools, for healthcare, for clean air and water, and responses to extreme weather events, will fall to the states. We must remain resilient. It’s not easy but we will work together. We will remember why we have a government - that pooling our resources is an efficient and cost effective way to provide services, educate our children, protect our environment and provide a safety net for the most vulnerable members of our communities. This past month, I have enjoyed meeting with many of you at meetings in our community and at two town halls. My next town hall is Monday, April 21 at the Reston Community Center at Lake Anne at 6:30 pm. I will be presenting commendations with Delegate Keys-Gamarra. I will have a second town hall to present commendations with Delegate Shin on May 21 at the Herndon Community Center at 6:30 pm. Please call my office or email if you need help with a state issue. Kindest regards, Jennifer B. BoyskoMember, Senate of Virginia Representing the newly created 38th Senate District making up the northernmost portions of Fairfax County (Great Falls, Herndon, McLean, Reston, WolfTrap, parts of unincorporated Vienna and a slice of Falls Church) PS If you would like to support my work, please visit =. Clients are qualified by their case managers and social workers; most are typically living in shelters or part of a transitional program in and around the DMV area. Many clients leave their crises with only the clothing on their backs. WGB helps them restock basic necessities free of charge. WGB is currently fundraising to expand their facility in Sterling. Transportation Updates Opportunity to Comment on Over $7 Billion of Planned Transportation Investments The Virginia Department of Rail and Public Transportation (DRPT) has released its draft Fiscal Year (FY) 2026–2031 Six-Year Improvement Program (SYIP), which outlines planned investments of $7 billion in public transportation, and freight and passenger rail across the Commonwealth over the next six fiscal years. The SYIP details DRPT’s funding priorities for FY 2026, allocating $332 million to local and regional public transportation providers, $442 million to WMATA’s Metrorail and Metrobus services, and $17 million to the Virginia Railway Express (VRE) operating and capital budget. The program also includes continued investment in freight rail for $20 million and $232 million for the Virginia Passenger Rail Authority to expand and sustain passenger rail service in Virginia. The draft program is now available for public review on . For additional resources, including how to submit a comment online and view the CTB public meeting schedule and locations, . Now available on YouTube: the Environmental and Energy Study Institute Congressional Camp Briefing on YouTube: I was on the panel speaking about the importance and impact of federal transportation funding in Virginia. The Surface Transportation bill is reauthorized every five years. Federal funding makes up 17% of . Funding for our highways, bridges, bike and pedestrian paths, and transit all depend on federal funding. For as long as I’ve been serving on the Senate Transportation Committee, the Northern Virginia Transportation Authority, and the Metropolitan Washington Council of Governments Transportation Planning Board, we have had more project proposals than available funding. Virginia is fortunate that we have systems in place to evaluate and weigh projects to ensure the greatest benefit from every transportation dollar spent. | | Constituent Services Resources for Those Impacted by DOGE Times are tough - there’s a lot going on. Federal workers laid off by the new federal administration are encouraged to reach out to their federal legislators - , and your . The is also gathering stories of people impacted by the DOGE cuts. Fairfax County has created a webpage with a variety of resources that can help former federal employees, contractors and anyone else affected by job loss. Services include searching and preparing for a new job with listings available through the County and a database maintained by the Fairfax County Economic Development Authority. Financial help is available for many health and human service programs, taxes and pet-related expenses. A link to the new webpage is included below: . The Fairfax County Economic Development Authority, with funding from the Fairfax County Board of Supervisors, has introduced a one-stop resource for connecting with hundreds of employers actively hiring in our region: . You can watch their . They have 90,000 jobs posted. For those eligible for unemployment benefits, the Virginia Employment Commission (VEC) requires that people receiving unemployment benefits show proof of job searches each week. I encourage you to seek support for finding a job from . They help identify training programs, job opportunities, and help with resumes, emails, and interviewing skills. My constituents report good results working with VCW. . is a initiative. The site includes suggestions for federal workers seeking to rewrite their resumes for the private sector, plus region-by-region links to major employers’ corporate career pages and links to region-specific job searches on Indeed and LinkedIn. Additional Resources for Federal Employees Facing Unemployment: NOVA New Employment, Exploration and Transition (NOVAnext) , is a new program to help recently unemployed federal workers and federal contractors transition to their next careers. The first in the region to provide new, dedicated training funds for unemployed federal workers and federal contractors, NOVA is committing up to $1 million in scholarship aid to allow approved participants to enroll in one of 14 courses at no charge and access NOVA-sponsored online workshops on resume-writing and job search techniques. Many classes prepare students for industry-recognized certifications in specific IT specialties, program management and human resources. To be eligible, federal workers and federal contract workers must have been laid off on or after February 1, 2025, reside within the NOVA service area, meet a residency requirement and fill out an online application, which will be reviewed by Virginia Career Works. For more information, visit If you are a government employee with information related to the politicization of the federal workforce, the firing of civil servants, or waste, fraud, or abuse of taxpayer dollars, you may to the Democrats on the U.S. House Oversight Committee. The U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission has resources regarding employment discrimination for federal employees that can be accessed . Employment rights under federal law (U.S. Office of Special Counsel) Whistleblower protections and accompanying resources , click To understand prohibited personnel practices including the right to appeal, visit The Library of Congress Beginner's Guide to Civil Service Employment Law can be found . Help Paying Bills If you are struggling to pay bills, please visit Virginia’s to figure out if you qualify for assistance. You can also reach out to your county - your local government will review your financial situation and connect you with resources. In Fairfax County, contact . are additional resources for food. If you have lost your health insurance, please visit to figure out if you are eligible for Medicaid or for help with Affordable Care Act insurance. If you cannot afford healthcare, visit . You can also find support for yourself and family members by reaching out to or. Please reach out if you need help for yourself or a loved one. As a member of the , we work together to ensure that people have the resources they need to identify and get help for addiction recovery. Early detection and treatment are key to recovery – support is a phone call away. For additional information, please call the Substance Use Disorder Prevention program case manager at 703-866-2121. Know Your Rights - Resources for Immigrants I will continue to work to ensure that our communities are welcoming, diverse and provide opportunities for all people to live with dignity and safety. Working together, we can build good lives for our families and our communities. The news of deportations is distressing and we can prepare. If you or a loved one encounter immigration enforcement officials, it is essential that you know your rights and have prepared your household for all possible outcomes : Ask for a warrant : The Fourth Amendment of the Constitution protects you from unreasonable search and seizure. You do not have to open your door until you see a valid warrant to enter your home or search your belongings. You have the right to remain silent : The Fifth Amendment protects your right to remain silent and not incriminate yourself. You are not required to share any personal information such as your place of birth, immigration status or criminal history. Respectfully say, “I am exercising my right to remain silent.” Always consult an attorney : You have a right to speak with an attorney. You do not have to sign anything or hand officials any documents without speaking to an attorney. Try to identify and consult one in advance. Please share this information from the ACLU, available in multiple languages: We are also working with the Legal Aid Justice Center which has asked that we share their . Additional Resources for immigrants: Fairfax County’s Fairfax County Public Schools: If you are an immigrant in need of legal services, please visit: Call Immigration Rapid Response Hotline, Virginia Network, 855-298-3271 in the event of an ICE raid or arrest For a list of resources provided by the Virginia Department of Social Services, please visit here: Opportunities April 19 - National Parks Free Day to visit! Entrance fees to national parks are waived on April 19, 2025, to kick off the celebration of National Park Week. Great Falls National Park is in our Senate District. Shenandoah National Park is a couple of hours away. Enjoy the opportunity to enjoy the outdoors this weekend. Get a head start on the Earth Day Celebration which is April 22 this year. Solarize NoVA Launches April 15 Since 2014 the campaign has resulted in more than 9 MW of new solar in region; $28+ Million in private sector investment The Northern Virginia Regional Commission, its local government partners and the Local Energy Alliance Program (LEAP) are pleased to announce this year’s Solarize NoVA campaign will kick off April 15. Since its inception in 2014, Solarize has been committed to supporting homeowners by offering free solar satellite assessments and education on the benefits of solar energy. The program has been widely successful, helping Northern Virginians save money and reduce their reliance on traditional energy sources. With the 2023 passing of the Inflation Reduction Act, the federal solar tax credit has increased from 26% to 30%, offering even more financial incentive for homeowners to make the switch to solar. As the nation navigates uncertainty, this may not always be the case. Homeowners and businesses interested in joining Solarize can sign up for a free solar satellite assessment and access discounted rates until July 15th. LEAP offers ongoing support throughout the process to ensure a seamless experience, from the initial consultation to installation. For more information on how to sign up for Solarize or to attend one of the free, informative webinars, please visit ArtsFairfax: Calling All Local Artists ArtsFairfax posts opportunities to apply for new commissions, various art performances, or exhibition opportunities from across the country, throughout the region, or from within the County. To view opportunities, please click . Empowering Virginia Educators Civics Navigator is a free resource provided by to help educators teach students about Virginia politics and government. With a range of materials tailored for K-12 classrooms, it offers lesson plans, data visualizations, and activities designed to engage students and develop their understanding of Virginia politics and government. Spread the Word: Know an educator who might benefit? ** with your network and help us support classrooms across Virginia. Give Back The Arc of Northern Virginia Benefit Breakfast is one of my favorite events each year - stories shared remind us of what we can accomplish with the combination of government funding and private donations, a clear purpose and need, and compassion. People with disabilities want “A Life Like Yours.” The Arc of Northern Virginia represents and serves more than 39,000 individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities (ID/DD) and their families with direct service programs, education, and advocacy. However, the struggle for quality of life for these thousands of individuals and their families is daunting. Virginia remains 39th among the states in providing community supports for citizens with ID/DD and services are limited for those who need costly lifelong support to continue to live in their communities. Even with the recent Medicaid expansion, more than 15,000 people remain on waiting lists for critical services. They need our help as we continue to work to ensure their full inclusion and participation in our communities. In the News Events Thursdays, May - October, 8 - noon/ at 765 Lynn St. in Herndon Fridays, May - October, 8 am - noon/ 1659 Chain Bridge Rd in McLean Saturdays, April 26 - November 22, 8 am - noon/ at 1609 Washington Plaza N in Reston Friday, May 2, 9 am – 7 pm/, 1479 Sterling Road in Herndon, Free. Attendees are encouraged to bring non-perishable food donations for communities in need. Thursday, May 15/, Join us at more than 100 pit stops in DC, MD, and VA for this FREE event for a fun and healthy way to start your day. The first 18,000 who register and attend at a pit stop receive a FREE T-shirt. Free giveaways, food, and beverages at participating locations, while supplies last. We welcome notices about events that are free or very low cost. Keep in Touch** I am back in District 38 with my Chief of Staff Karen Harrison, and aides, Kathy Shupe, and Juliet Morro. For constituent services and to schedule meetings with your community group, please call (703) 437-0086 or email Senator Boysko@senate.virginia.gov. Friend me on and follow me on | | If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe. name: Maggie Goodlander
subject: Looking forward
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for Congress
body:
Thank you from the bottom of my heart for putting your trust in me.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106026254/540178189/2145345036?refcode=BLU_EM_GOOD_2025-04-17_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMTMwMjAiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMzgyN2E2ODctY2QxYi1mMDExLThiM2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=vWQQP_vQVZNjzlZq_4T_roVvb9q3yOg3yB4I56SWef0=)
---
| Friends,

Humility isn't a feeling easily expressed with words. Neither is gratitude. But today, I feel both deeply. I am humbled by the encouragement I have received from so many Granite Staters to consider a run for the United States Senate. Thank you from the bottom of my heart for putting your trust in me.

After a period of reflection, I have decided to seek re-election to the United States House of Representatives.

This is a moment of profound crisis and challenge in our country as the President of the United States leads a cruel assault on the basic foundations of our Constitutional order and, in many cases, basic human decency. So, for me, the question boils down to this: How can I best serve this state and our country in this pivotal moment?

First, by doing the job I hold right now – a job I’ve truly loved doing every day for the past 104 days – to the best of my ability. That means devoting everything I’ve got to delivering for the people of the Second District and standing up to the cascading outrages and cruelties of the Trump Administration.

Second, by helping to secure a Democratic majority in the United States House of Representatives on November 3, 2026. Taking back the House is the most consequential thing we can do to check the excesses of President Trump and chart the path forward for New Hampshire and America.

To address the urgent mission at hand, we must unite in a common cause and in relentless defense of the promise and values on which our great country was founded.

So I look forward to the work ahead, and to keep earning the trust and support of the people of New Hampshire. We’re in this together, so I hope you’ll join our people-powered team. Click [_**here**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106026255/540178191/1211068418?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMTMwMjAiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMzgyN2E2ODctY2QxYi1mMDExLThiM2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=vWQQP_vQVZNjzlZq_4T_roVvb9q3yOg3yB4I56SWef0=&emci=02d6868b-c61b-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=3827a687-cd1b-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=10367155) to sign up for updates from our campaign and find out how you can get involved.

Onward,

Maggie

name: Katie Hobbs
subject: Why I do this work
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Nevada State Democratic Party, NVDEMS
body:
| | | Friend, I want to take a moment to share a little bit about my journey with you — because at the heart of everything I do is a deep commitment to service. But before I dive in, my campaign is facing an important fundraising deadline next week. [**_We need to hit our end-of-month goal to stay on track, and I’d be grateful if you could split a donation between NV Dems and our campaign today._**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqu4ksFa5SF7IXH6Sr7Lz9yTmdiQdzoky2q0uQUqnPbOqXEe6Oyk8Sw56-YL5ALRrRQN0KVFQxBTXBZQsJWl0a7AemYPce3XWvDDRdGjflvOekT9yxI0hNrhfXbZcGJ2-4UqrzP5SlJgN8y6Jp4RErJ_rMpwrIRXiUQzqeXJ107STc_CpQvV4nfT184muljtMAIT5ZIpjl_1pmxYLC0uGTJ16DFvff9hWXQeuG8Y7B_uCtI6K_Hq_H_JkpeU0NLlp3ooPOdNKTE3r4u3tfdMJaFoKJkcEOPNivXVBnK5NktNyE3c_QnMtYAp82g8kNCchOOHxlSFRDOxci_rvb8h7zcLXLhPofgTuRt9q0zdTUCWx_XCq-CjuIYpvpT89OfKiHU/4fq/bZNaklSqS0KGMSYPt7f_yQ/h0/h001.IIVHmu0dy2Vv9qUDD3kwKY72fFnccui8l2aTQ2SbwH4) | [Donate](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqu4ksFa5SF7IXH6Sr7Lz9yTmdiQdzoky2q0uQUqnPbOqXEe6Oyk8Sw56-YL5ALRrRQN0KVFQxBTXBZQsJWl0a7AcP4BXc7ZjGC3jrDplN6abSmxk3t5PEbQJhLxwYktaD1Vz_yMOJaCH15edHBxaO2JZqu5TB24Ug4dG6qmFyJqYHyLTYIDfDQR38Xseskbkws4lQ4_2651kKCsc31OPMPZfccODtupAhpUkbPFIFQQCRVAMU0MAtLG1UkeoJDVFdiyGYHxvuiC20ZBhetyULGk4xxcfd-vDHV3MohrC6YZ3rbJ1O5tXlC4snte1lKR18fSwOKltQmbiqOZcyAqvGu6EYi5Vkl7elKJii8vxea6Ne5aqNmbarL1LNAmB4hUZYQ/4fq/bZNaklSqS0KGMSYPt7f_yQ/h1/h001.3t7m_zV8MyhBh9R4nwWyLZYkPgVTq4_SsqDCg8zrWJ4)
---
Growing up in Tempe, my family — like so many others — had its fair share of struggles. We faced financial uncertainty, and at times, we had to rely on food assistance and support from our church to make ends meet.
[ ](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqu4ksFa5SF7IXH6Sr7Lz9yTmdiQdzoky2q0uQUqnPbOqXEe6Oyk8Sw56-YL5ALRrRQN0KVFQxBTXBZQsJWl0a7Ay6lznFa7nTmg_tuKBzhBWMczty2qp_bly9Y95GC0fFHVrCv_fbmnHvii8hX4FP4zIF5DCSvS9cUUSkTB-5ENH6T7B9W1WWFayIT1_mxEevNkXHc-TlvLu8kqO2vDKw6LU9JuN4qR-ZDoAsvEGIowWJqlO3IjGe-R35Ie5jM_nv3b-1f40vvxv_GrBqQNs4uSVkWVIavv2C-quFou-JoQhskQMuldwJdYNdwRY1d8GgmET3CIt1SzGWfXfu9oxTJ--8zxVkPUChePzGl92g1xJLTmeTD0-s4qZV23qV3yOxg/4fq/bZNaklSqS0KGMSYPt7f_yQ/h2/h001.H5BYUyKfbxZrurjG4cZC2v4FAfWNeOjqcYI4NrkAJjw)
---
My parents worked hard to provide for me and my twin sister, and we were lucky to have a community that stepped up when we needed it most. That experience shaped who I am today. It taught me the power of public service, genuine caring for your neighbors, and the importance of giving back.
That’s why I started volunteering at my church, then became a social worker, and helped run one of the largest domestic violence shelters in the country. I saw firsthand how families were struggling — not because they weren’t working hard, but because the system wasn’t working for them.
That's what led me to run for office. I refused to accept that so many Arizonans had to fight just to get by while politicians ignored their struggles. So I ran, and I haven’t lost an election since.
Now, as Arizona’s 24th Governor, I’m fighting to make sure no family is left behind. I’m working to lower the cost of housing and healthcare, expand opportunities for working families, and ensure every Arizonan can thrive.
**But I can’t do this alone. Extreme Republicans are working to unseat me and block our progress at every turn, and I need your help.[_Can you split a donation today and help us reach our end-of-month goal? Every contribution — no matter the amount — makes a huge difference._](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqu4ksFa5SF7IXH6Sr7Lz9yTmdiQdzoky2q0uQUqnPbOqXEe6Oyk8Sw56-YL5ALRrRQN0KVFQxBTXBZQsJWl0a7Aw7vjTwDmeIz9qmdA61e0qKgfddV2nK0uI_FwvRwlogzMqyheWufF47gBB59jq_OfMyzGmUScrOzA-DzMtXvdDB9ksNyrE6S1FKawJjFG6zDPGM_FgtebiiNJkVF2fz5gMqRlsRQfO-9t4XrRNL8q0ZwOYo7toeeWkhF6cRWq_FZFqlQeHMb7nLgsm7pvKjRC4XhlFY-gS-vIj0LKQOUkauxTLjPrweHvxKIWlt2JgTkm0jAhL8ek1D8tx-f_ZCOWC1J3PVLwHoW5bK2oOhAvQfvozo9LgbeAJ0HaHuTale8/4fq/bZNaklSqS0KGMSYPt7f_yQ/h3/h001.1mDooy-y6Ku8OapW2NENt1lU9soUzWSAVwlCA56n-Kg)**

clean_body: | | | Friend, I want to take a moment to share a little bit about my journey with you — because at the heart of everything I do is a deep commitment to service. But before I dive in, my campaign is facing an important fundraising deadline next week. | Growing up in Tempe, my family — like so many others — had its fair share of struggles. We faced financial uncertainty, and at times, we had to rely on food assistance and support from our church to make ends meet. My parents worked hard to provide for me and my twin sister, and we were lucky to have a community that stepped up when we needed it most. That experience shaped who I am today. It taught me the power of public service, genuine caring for your neighbors, and the importance of giving back. That’s why I started volunteering at my church, then became a social worker, and helped run one of the largest domestic violence shelters in the country. I saw firsthand how families were struggling — not because they weren’t working hard, but because the system wasn’t working for them. That's what led me to run for office. I refused to accept that so many Arizonans had to fight just to get by while politicians ignored their struggles. So I ran, and I haven’t lost an election since. Now, as Arizona’s 24th Governor, I’m fighting to make sure no family is left behind. I’m working to lower the cost of housing and healthcare, expand opportunities for working families, and ensure every Arizonan can thrive. But I can’t do this alone. Extreme Republicans are working to unseat me and block our progress at every turn, and I need your help. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | ---|---|--- | | Thank you for taking the time to read my story today. I’m so grateful to have you by my side in this fight. — Katie The Nevada State Democratic Party fights for progress in the Silver State. We believe that we are stronger together than on our own, and that America succeeds when everyone gets a fair shot, everyone does their fair share, and everyone plays by the same rules. | | Paid for by the Nevada State Democratic Party, NVDEMS.com, not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. Peter — Do _**not**_ believe Republicans when they tell you they won’t cut Social Security. On the campaign trail, Donald Trump promised voters he wouldn’t touch Social Security. But less than 100 days into his term, he’s already shut down countless offices across the country and fired staff. His top adviser, unelected billionaire Elon Musk, called Social Security a “Ponzi scheme.” Republicans in Congress aren’t any better. I recently proposed an amendment to prevent any cuts to Social Security. **Peter,_every_ Republican on the Budget and Rules Committees voted against it — despite how much they _claim_ that they won’t touch Social Security.** **[If Republicans can’t agree to take cuts to Social Security off the table to pay for their tax breaks for billionaires, that should tell you _a lot_ about who they’re working for. clean_body: | = Peter — Do not believe Republicans when they tell you they won’t cut Social Security. On the campaign trail, Donald Trump promised voters he wouldn’t touch Social Security. But less than 100 days into his term, he’s already shut down countless offices across the country and fired staff. His top adviser, unelected billionaire Elon Musk, called Social Security a “Ponzi scheme.” Republicans in Congress aren’t any better. I recently proposed an amendment to prevent any cuts to Social Security. Peter,every Republican on the Budget and Rules Committees voted against it — despite how much they claim that they won’t touch Social Security. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = Social Security is an essential lifeline for seniors who have worked hard and deserve to retire with dignity. name: Mark Warner
subject: When the SAVE Act comes up in the Senate, I will vote no.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner
body:
Will you stand with me against this blatant assault on our democracy?

|
---

| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106027246/540181359/215467254?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_SAVEAct_250417_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyNWZkMTA5MS1jZjFiLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KLbZU5LMv5m5qM6VjaCGIOwvSUcm7IxM3jWyLeIL6QI=) Derek,

My office has received hundreds upon hundreds of calls in the past week about the SAVE Act – the bill coming to the Senate that House Republicans passed to make it harder for MILLIONS of Americans to vote.

**This bill would require you to show proof of U.S. citizenship – such as a passport or a birth certificate – _in person_ when registering to vote or updating your voter registration.**

Derek, did you know that 146 million American citizens don’t own a passport? Or that tens of millions of spouses won’t be able to vote if they changed their name on their ID and it doesn’t match their birth certificate? And that nearly 60 million rural residents would have to drive far from their homes _just_ to become eligible to vote?

**Republicans can claim that this bill is to “combat voting fraud,” but we see it for what it really is: a transparent attack on voting rights and an assault on our democracy.**

**So let me be clear: when the SAVE Act comes up in the Senate, I will vote no.**

I’ll do whatever is necessary to stop this bill and protect every eligible voter’s fundamental right to access the ballot box.

[**_This bill is an unfortunate reminder that we MUST take back the Senate next year – so a Democratic majority can stop Republicans from rolling back our fundamental rights and serve as an important check on Trump’s reckless agenda. If you are able to, please join the fight today with a donation to my reelection campaign >>>_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106027247/540181381/215467254?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_SAVEAct_250417_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyNWZkMTA5MS1jZjFiLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KLbZU5LMv5m5qM6VjaCGIOwvSUcm7IxM3jWyLeIL6QI=)

The fight continues,

Mark Warner**** **__********[CONTRIBUTE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106027248/540181398/215467254?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_SAVEAct_250417_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyNWZkMTA5MS1jZjFiLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KLbZU5LMv5m5qM6VjaCGIOwvSUcm7IxM3jWyLeIL6QI=)********__**

clean_body: You just won the Gold Medal in Trump World! Please accept your award... | | | | Friend, Congratulations! You just won the Gold Medal in Trump World. Honoring you with the Golden Trump Award is my way of saying THANK YOU for your unwavering support! JD Vance received it. Top Trump Cabinet members received it. Now it’s YOUR TURN. ** Only 1% of supporters will EVER be chosen. That’s why I’m sending you a LIMITED-EDITION TRUMP FLAG PIN to mark this historic moment — but only if you accept your award BEFORE MIDNIGHT. WE’RE SHIPPING YOU THIS GOLDEN TRUMP BADGE AS PROOF YOU MADE THE CUT! ** Friend, once again, thank you so much for your support, and congratulations! Sincerely, President Donald J. name: Ruben Gallego
subject: Town Halls and Accountability
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Gallego for Arizona
body:
It really bothers me that Republicans are ramming through a budget that’s going to lower taxes on billionaires and destroy programs that millions of regular folks depend on to pay for it.
#

Peter, it's Ruben.
I’ve been doing town halls across Arizona over the last two months. To me, it’s one of the most important parts of the job.
This has been a part of my time in public life ever since I first got elected. Last summer, when it was well over 100 degrees every day, I was out there in communities meeting people face-to-face. I’d often talk to people that disagreed with me, and when I did I’d still take the time to listen.
That’s why I’m going out there. It’s not about scoring political points. It’s what is demanded of you. And it really bothers me that Republicans are ramming through a budget that’s going to lower taxes on billionaires and destroy programs that millions of regular folks depend on to pay for it. All while Trump’s making billions and running the most corrupt administration in history.
That’s the explicit position of the National Republican Party today — and most of their leaders aren’t even willing to go back to the people they represent to answer for it.
As for me, I have a town hall on the Yavapai-Apache Nation Reservation today, and another out in Flagstaff tomorrow. I’ve been out there. I’ll continue to be out there.
I owe the people of Arizona that.
Thanks for having my back. I will never forget that I’m serving in the Senate because of you and thousands of other supporters. It means a lot to me.
Semper Fi,
Ruben
🌵 Please! Don’t click away yet 🌞
While Ruben takes on the Trump administration, he needs to know he has the support of a big team behind him. If you can afford it, [**consider making a donation to his campaign to help him keep up the pressure.**](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1000?c0=ruben-em-apr25.dU9j8y&refcode=em250417-1-A&t=1&akid=3824%2E734936%2E0Lip6r)
[CONTRIBUTE](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1000?c0=ruben-em-apr25.dU9j8y&refcode=em250417-1-A&t=2&akid=3824%2E734936%2E0Lip6r)
Thank you! 🫡

Paid For By Gallego for Arizona

name: Ritchie Torres
subject: This is who I am, Peter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Torres For Congress PO Box 580303, Bronx, NY 10458
body:
| [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106027814/540183171/1654258770?refcode=header-img&amount=25&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NjE2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMmFmODkwNS1kMTFiLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=URtkl02EWqIcJTe1QkLsoxJ0Y0wfp8uXhq4lB71QNXg=)

Peter, I'm not your typical Member of Congress:

I don't have mega donors on speed-dial. I don't come from a wealthy family with political connections. I don't even have a college degree.

**THIS is who I am:**

I shocked everyone and won as a progressive in an election against a hateful anti-LGBTQ candidate. I was then elected as the first gay, Afro-Latino member of Congress.

I was born and raised in The Bronx by a single working mom who worked multiple jobs. We lived in public housing and struggled in poverty.

I refuse to do "call-time,” which is political jargon for dialing up ultra-rich mega-donors and begging them for donations.

I spend my time working on the issues that matter to my constituents, like ending poverty, advocating for affordable housing, protecting their rights, and improving their quality of life.

**It's not easy to run a campaign on these values, Peter — but it's the RIGHT way.**

[So I'm humbly asking you now for a $3 contribution to my re-election campaign. My team wants to raise $25,000 this month to fuel my fight and take back the House, but at the current pace, I don't know if we'll make it. name: Vice President JD Vance
subject: Raised by my Mamaw
disclaimer_text: Paid for by TEAM SCALISE, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and to benefit SCALISE FOR CONGRESS and EYE OF THE TIGER PAC
body:
This is a longer email but I respectfully ask you to read it start to finish:

| |
---
| | | | [](http://links.conservativeum.com/s/c/2XFuBWD3E9_qshYpk1_KcMAgP5ntjvhEDTZIQz4N8MgC8grIVEMFoGXzHPPwXc-o715VEn1fis1WfX0d2qbh8hapTJ-YZjMmXjOeZczrrogDwxRQ9U8z7PLX27LQ2yAcl-Zu53-xunMlBYflWzjppVIA4kg-x1btHHK7lghxmNysD3HWgi_fbjsgHlhXzrZWlg-dWflt5zFfoFCefais32I14L9etgsN2oj1_HAkMz-9dZGn8AyUemlxUFpkqrHZClHOf2BnBoyCdiFhc8Y5UimMB_Kr5-PXki3mc0kHeeWWzqDtrDdEJXMMCkcfiJV01N1ek68rHfPcnKdh7VOWePX_Yvnp5CpHYuHdIqRAmaEgcG_XQxdWG0T-WQTG8LHL7hAf9Bl1LGyru4XHYylJAYV85hwOJ9mLL4qDsZpR0jTBi9CvQfRHxGyC6mMLDgEgboqn7pVIT9NUivNFjYc_Jaa5Yhftwz6secmmjdN93A/cNsjXvR5rp52-Fu-EW0v2Ua0h2butJFx/8 "Vice President JD Vance")
---

My fellow Americans - it is Vice President JD Vance.

_**[This is a longer email but I respectfully ask you to read it from start to finish.](http://links.conservativeum.com/s/c/m6jMaxf6NAcrZPTbJQqLRhSDy-CfGziCKs815OhNqUUZ2KDhoiR2xmmfX26QzWyZyMC2gl0lJWHaOxLxdQYkFrNsY0nfqettyDgzYodr_RSJvRr-9A4GFTm8EGBGldSu58pkfEp_E-EjnYm0PWhS65IMusjpf1qnob9_EUYlgtfkLh1wJOte31JAHGr9mXBCTDEcBFkjXvWukkHtHYrnzVKUV1VaglCxy6zMWuCG0HY1endmt4A-B6WhqCkynYitvxlBPF0qomTm_aN2vESClwU07jfbcklZKN4llcXhMVaynAEVm_12yqrEx37xuBo0DYA3kboHSDKd33sR5K13As_TbyyMPn18kOtFswvZVNpjyYyJZpxEJHh1gyhSGdsBLVELmGWzqVRvkUoz5WJmfMvYMkMOt_oZIgqEnTC7znfh7tHYSQ434O9etieV-s65SjjN3AJEbKSGN1MUYNrMVyFPYNFImgEtiZpXVSQ0kA/w6O_fTB59Jc2Tt_uzAUVQJmBIEWtiw3m/8 "This is a longer email but I respectfully ask you to read it from start to finish.")**_

It wasn't easy telling the world I grew up poor, raised by my Mamaw while my mother struggled with addiction.

It wasn't exactly the prep school/trust fund/summer-in-the-Hamptons upbringing that is commonplace in DC.

**But I felt called to tell my story. Warts and all.** I felt led to speak out because I think millions of Americans share stories like mine. Stories of jobs being outsourced to China, addiction ravaging communities and killing our neighbors, and heeding the call of duty after the Towers fell on 9/11.

_As your Vice President, I now stand in the breach for the left-behind, forgotten men and women who built this country, but were abandoned by the political class in DC. _

I'll do everything in my power to make sure that never happens again. I'll fight every day for good folks like you.

I promise to always stand between you and the Democrat Machine that aims to obliterate everything that makes our country great.

_**[Which brings me to the purpose of my email today.](http://links.conservativeum.com/s/c/H5ltIkYJw24Pr6Yfg2NEjT-aHms9j1BPnsuxrj0id38arfp16zUBOKh49w_1NvVKOz8-kfD4GD7B4uWoRSxEuw0fE7-7VlygAYBQE2pv5N5kZmR9lnik_GRJdI8cl7JALPwrzBcwEOMQbsJKBfSYvaJ0hP8uWtfHwPWB1OmCbwh-hQrZmbNy5EF_vdyvf1gH_y5dv-7U0NjuaUyuvvTbpkyNpsr6dMydzbTjUEpGOgrRnxfxUHzwuvkVv-KoVzpkGMIHk-2lk-pjxYDOXDOTs7F2-LXTAIEBdSzIWLvvoBCgFguuLeJwjZ2-VMIskfRrW49gOS4urc4SCOjipfBhpq2DJ-faZh_Dw_r8SooIiYViBYJL9WkCAUxl2J_pmUkuHg_S2DqToqxQ_FEbHqopPJGAHqh3NTo0SuIsm_6_eGeM3O_yr5dnLePTzxUh_JkB1XwyIG1-8LkBI2RY8077XZE0ypxbfNei-2ZzIl7hRQ/wAKaqinlRWTDcoi7qR_fw1I-2vwDf9kx/8 "Which brings me to the purpose of my email today.")**_

We're coming out of the most expensive election in American history. I mean that. Kamala Harris spent over a billion dollars against us. It took EVERY PENNY we had to get our story out there, expose her ultra-Liberal record, reach undecided voters, and win.

So now we're forced to rebuild.

The Democrat Machine is **ALREADY** crushing us in fundraising, **ALREADY** outspending us in key races, and **ALREADY** building the campaign infrastructure to decimate us in the Midterm Elections.

At this point, the consequences of simply resting on our laurels would be unthinkable.

So here's my final pitch to you:

_**[I'm teaming up with Majority Leader Steve Scalise - for the FIRST TIME since the election - to raise a million dollars by the end of the month.](http://links.conservativeum.com/s/c/Uxajx92BgBbLcuWP9tyIm1JavMmBzEEGUVWbr0Mb7qu78pKIUyRCd4j74JUT3HI3W6JMpAEV5a6Ma8D9B-ihFGzanyNprbfn3FUwdTnYGl2LWI6LD7aUjWysz0AxjbRrvb1-WcVkpe9OdG8NdyY2ajwmdfSIl7EZengc5x8aZZUDDCKxyu6UtCWpT_oZXoPA6AX0uPGBrcxsa25PbtV8hLrA8phn_2ClLcT8Vz-p4zwvBVhO0m-s2XuKU7BdI2ryQK67zFOzOBtYZWaPDk7n3_ijUvmtgmgQRec54KFnL1qWGV8SN2ujaofrkXpTDxHcWf8dUuPpAqiDuOC7Gb9CRiKFWpKzAF6sOnDBSaB4AmP6I_bEarka-pSXvSfQ-FwZHjSjnlS-6xMOk-VpXfo3tk7kEHlABdWxQX95ZY58G2wXr2PJDsAG5pacnf9BWGIPBDkZ07BoQcvJ7h6LEKBIm6WEG515pIFksnxsGpazyQ/937oKkiYoqhMhR7FIRq5Pyyd1AipCZ5b/8 "I’m teaming up with Majority Leader Steve Scalise – for the FIRST TIME since the election – to raise a million dollars by the end of the month.")**_ I understand that's ambitious. But if that's what it takes to deliver President Trump a Republican House for his entire second term, it's a no-brainer I'll do what it takes to win.

Do you think you can help us get there?

_Whether it 's $5 or $500 - the amount you give isn't nearly as important as how many folks step up:_
---

clean_body: This is a longer email but I respectfully ask you to read it start to finish: | | | | | | "Vice President JD Vance" My fellow Americans - it is Vice President JD Vance. "This is a longer email but I respectfully ask you to read it from start to finish." It wasn't easy telling the world I grew up poor, raised by my Mamaw while my mother struggled with addiction. It wasn't exactly the prep school/trust fund/summer-in-the-Hamptons upbringing that is commonplace in DC. But I felt called to tell my story. Warts and all. I felt led to speak out because I think millions of Americans share stories like mine. Stories of jobs being outsourced to China, addiction ravaging communities and killing our neighbors, and heeding the call of duty after the Towers fell on 9/11. As your Vice President, I now stand in the breach for the left-behind, forgotten men and women who built this country, but were abandoned by the political class in DC. I'll do everything in my power to make sure that never happens again. I'll fight every day for good folks like you. I promise to always stand between you and the Democrat Machine that aims to obliterate everything that makes our country great. "Which brings me to the purpose of my email today." We're coming out of the most expensive election in American history. I mean that. Kamala Harris spent over a billion dollars against us. It took EVERY PENNY we had to get our story out there, expose her ultra-Liberal record, reach undecided voters, and win. So now we're forced to rebuild. The Democrat Machine is ALREADY crushing us in fundraising, ALREADY outspending us in key races, and ALREADY building the campaign infrastructure to decimate us in the Midterm Elections. At this point, the consequences of simply resting on our laurels would be unthinkable. So here's my final pitch to you: "I’m teaming up with Majority Leader Steve Scalise – for the FIRST TIME since the election – to raise a million dollars by the end of the month." I understand that's ambitious. But if that's what it takes to deliver President Trump a Republican House for his entire second term, it's a no-brainer I'll do what it takes to win. Do you think you can help us get there? Whether it 's $5 or $500 - the amount you give isn't nearly as important as how many folks step up: | | | "PLEASE JOIN US" Look, you've probably heard bits and pieces of my story at this point. If my humble beginnings taught me anything, it's to never give up, never surrender, and never go down without a fight. That's why I'm partnering with Steve Scalise to help lay the critical groundwork we need to win. "I don’t ever want you to donate more than you can afford – but if you can spare ANYTHING, even a few bucks, it would be tremendously appreciated." Regardless, it's an honor to fight alongside you. name: Care in Action
subject: Last chance: Submit a message to care workers today.
body:
Check out our recap from the We Power Care Assembly.
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/106028818/540186525/664783965?ms=041725_assemblywrap&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNV08vMS83NjczNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlZDBjNGUxZS1kNTFiLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2pw1JeecNzPuS2cHm8oIYg0CTLX4F78W_dxzx-t6cl8=&emci=97733222-b11b-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=ed0c4e1e-d51b-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=864787)
Hi Peter,
Last week, Care in Action and the National Domestic Workers Alliance hosted the **We Power Care 2025 Assembly** —and it was more than just a gathering. It was a rallying cry.
Bringing together **nearly 1,500 domestic worker leaders, organizers, and affiliate partners** from across the country, the Assembly was a powerful ignition point—refueling our movement with energy, clarity, and purpose for the fights ahead.
From electrifying speeches on the future of care, the role of AI in caregiving, and worker rights, to **60 high-impact workshops** designed to sharpen our strategies and our skills, every moment was rooted in action and vision. A vibrant Culture Night reminded us why we fight: for joy, dignity, and recognition.
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/106028819/540186527/-1052118215?ms=041725_assemblywrap&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNV08vMS83NjczNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlZDBjNGUxZS1kNTFiLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2pw1JeecNzPuS2cHm8oIYg0CTLX4F78W_dxzx-t6cl8=&emci=97733222-b11b-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=ed0c4e1e-d51b-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=864787)
Among the featured guests, **Senator Bernie Sanders** spoke to the power of care workers. Our community even got the chance to ask Bernie questions!
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/106028820/540186528/-1052118215?ms=041725_assemblywrap&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNV08vMS83NjczNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlZDBjNGUxZS1kNTFiLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2pw1JeecNzPuS2cHm8oIYg0CTLX4F78W_dxzx-t6cl8=&emci=97733222-b11b-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=ed0c4e1e-d51b-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=864787)
This wasn’t just a weekend of inspiration—it was a turning point. We closed the Assembly with a fierce call to action and a bold roadmap for the future of care work. Our path is clear, and our people are ready.
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/106028821/540186529/-1052118215?ms=041725_assemblywrap&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNV08vMS83NjczNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlZDBjNGUxZS1kNTFiLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2pw1JeecNzPuS2cHm8oIYg0CTLX4F78W_dxzx-t6cl8=&emci=97733222-b11b-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=ed0c4e1e-d51b-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=864787)
Photos by Stefan A. Photography
**The We Power Care 2025 Assembly lit the fire—and now, we’re taking that fire into every community, every campaign, and every corner of this country.**
You can still join the celebration by adding a message to our _Power Capsule_ time capsule, which will be opened in five years. This is your last chance to [**_share a message_**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/106028822/540186530/-1052118215?ms=041725_assemblywrap&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNV08vMS83NjczNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlZDBjNGUxZS1kNTFiLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2pw1JeecNzPuS2cHm8oIYg0CTLX4F78W_dxzx-t6cl8=&emci=97733222-b11b-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=ed0c4e1e-d51b-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=864787) of hope, encouragement, and reflection for care workers, so be sure to submit yours today!
**Here are some example messages from our community:**
_“Your sensitive, wise touch gives me hope that I'll heal, will live a while longer in this beautiful world; I know that alone, isolated, ill, I would perish. Thank you a zillion times for being you! Keep on, dear one(s)!” _
\- Jeanne _“A housekeeper is also someone's precious family member.”_
-Kim D. _“Our society cannot survive without YOU!”_
-George P.

Thank you for sharing your message — be sure to [**_read all of the messages_**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/106028825/540186533/1289708337?ms=041725_assemblywrap&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNV08vMS83NjczNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlZDBjNGUxZS1kNTFiLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2pw1JeecNzPuS2cHm8oIYg0CTLX4F78W_dxzx-t6cl8=&emci=97733222-b11b-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=ed0c4e1e-d51b-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=864787) from supporters like you. clean_body: Check out our recap from the We Power Care Assembly. Hi Peter, Last week, Care in Action and the National Domestic Workers Alliance hosted the We Power Care 2025 Assembly —and it was more than just a gathering. It was a rallying cry. Bringing together nearly 1,500 domestic worker leaders, organizers, and affiliate partners from across the country, the Assembly was a powerful ignition point—refueling our movement with energy, clarity, and purpose for the fights ahead. From electrifying speeches on the future of care, the role of AI in caregiving, and worker rights, to 60 high-impact workshops designed to sharpen our strategies and our skills, every moment was rooted in action and vision. A vibrant Culture Night reminded us why we fight: for joy, dignity, and recognition. Among the featured guests, Senator Bernie Sanders spoke to the power of care workers. Our community even got the chance to ask Bernie questions! This wasn’t just a weekend of inspiration—it was a turning point. We closed the Assembly with a fierce call to action and a bold roadmap for the future of care work. Our path is clear, and our people are ready. Photos by Stefan A. Photography The We Power Care 2025 Assembly lit the fire—and now, we’re taking that fire into every community, every campaign, and every corner of this country. You can still join the celebration by adding a message to our Power Capsule time capsule, which will be opened in five years. This is your last chance to of hope, encouragement, and reflection for care workers, so be sure to submit yours today! Here are some example messages from our community: “Your sensitive, wise touch gives me hope that I'll heal, will live a while longer in this beautiful world; I know that alone, isolated, ill, I would perish. Thank you a zillion times for being you! Keep on, dear one(s)!” \- Jeanne “A housekeeper is also someone's precious family member.” -Kim D. “Our society cannot survive without YOU!” -George P. **************** Thank you for sharing your message — be sure to from supporters like you. Our community is stronger together, especially when we recognize the important work of care. Care in Action

name: Team Jayapal
subject: We must ALL speak out against these crimes
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
This situation is exactly why Pramila started the Resistance Labs.
# [](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/892?refcode=em20250417-4957-footer&t=1&akid=4957%2E420207%2Elenfoi)
Peter, if the masks weren’t off before, they’re definitely off now.
Donald Trump is revoking student visas for the “crime” of supporting a ceasefire in Gaza. He is illegally disappearing U.S. residents despite court-ordered protections against their deportation. And now he wants to send American citizens to notorious prisons in El Salvador too:
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-20250417?refcode=em20250417-4957&amount=3&refcode2=4957_420207_lenfoi&akid=4957%2E420207%2Elenfoi)
**Our democracy and our rights are under immediate threat, and it’s incumbent upon ALL of us to speak out against these crimes and resist Trump’s authoritarian agenda.**
[**If you can, please rush a $3 donation to support the on-the-ground organizing Pramila is doing to fight back.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-20250417?refcode=em20250417-4957&amount=3&refcode2=4957_420207_lenfoi&akid=4957%2E420207%2Elenfoi)
Unfortunately, this situation is exactly why Pramila launched the Resistance Lab grassroots trainings: because she knows we can’t afford to sit this moment out. Democracies die in a matter of months, and it’s critical that we get as many people as possible prepared to resist.
Thousands of people have attended the Resistance Lab so far, where we’ve heard from experts about what democratic collapse looks like in other countries and which methods of resistance have proven to work.
Given the scale of the threat we’re facing, we need to be as prepared as possible. We already have plans for a new Resistance Lab training coming soon, and we’ll make sure you get the details as soon as they’re finalized.
**But doing this kind of organizing takes resources, and as a grassroots-funded campaign, we’re counting on your support to keep it up.**
[**Peter, if you can, will you pitch in $3 today to support Pramila’s organizing efforts and help us fight back against Trump’s authoritarian agenda?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-20250417?refcode=em20250417-4957&amount=3&refcode2=4957_420207_lenfoi&akid=4957%2E420207%2Elenfoi)
**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-20250417?amount=3&refcode=em20250417-4957&refcode2=4957_420207_lenfoi&akid=4957%2E420207%2Elenfoi)**
In solidarity,
Team Jayapal
**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-footer?refcode=em20250417-4957-footer&t=2&refcode2=4957_420207_lenfoi&akid=4957%2E420207%2Elenfoi)**

**As a state legislator,** Yadira Caraveo took on the corporate bullies and entrenched special interests. She helped pass historic legislation to lower prescription drug prices and protect our communities against toxic pollution.

**As Colorado’s first Latina Congresswoman,** Yadira Caraveo fought to protect abortion access, defend affordable health care, and address the high cost of living for working families.

**Now, she’s running to win back this seat** because our current representative, Gabe Evans, is making life harder for working families to give tax cuts to billionaires.

So Peter, will you add your signature here and sign on to support Yadira? | Thank you, Team Caraveo for Congress | | | = Make sure this email goes to your inbox. Add info@caraveoforcongress.com to your address book. This campaign is powered by grassroots donations. You can = to make a donation to support Yadira Caraveo for CO-08. Email is a key way for us to stay in touch and make sure you get the latest updates from our campaign. But if you no longer want to receive email updates, | Paid for by Caraveo for Congress Caraveo for Congress PO Box 953 Eastlake, CO 80614 United States

name: Becky Edwards
subject: Why Does Civility Matter?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY GOVERNING GROUP PAC
body:
With passion high throughout our country and state, we believe that civil dialogue helps guide good policy.

| | | | | [](https://beckyforutah.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9cc818aa94e94ffd36a8547b3&id=3cefe5179f&e=6484bb6dd5)
---
| | | | | Dear Friend,[Last week, I had the honor of being featured on _RadioWest_](https://beckyforutah.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9cc818aa94e94ffd36a8547b3&id=e3cfd33af7&e=6484bb6dd5) —one of Utah’s most respected and widely listened-to radio programs. In a wide-ranging conversation, we didn’t chat much about politics. Instead, we talked about listening. I was able to share what Governing Group is all about. We don’t ask candidates to pass a specific policy test.**We believe that candidates can bring their deeply held beliefs to the table—AND still engage with open minds and open hearts.** And it’s working. Last November, **25 of the 28 candidates we endorsed won—and are now serving their communities and our state.** Voters responded to candidates who led with civility, not outrage—substance, not soundbites.But here’s the truth: this movement only works if people like you get involved. If you’re tired of performative politics, if you believe our communities deserve leaders who solve problems—not just score points—then this is your moment.
---
| [CHIP IN A DONATION](https://beckyforutah.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9cc818aa94e94ffd36a8547b3&id=920ca852bf&e=6484bb6dd5)
---
| [APPLY TO BE A 2025 CANDIDATE](https://beckyforutah.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9cc818aa94e94ffd36a8547b3&id=014b7ad26f&e=6484bb6dd5)
---
| Change doesn’t start in Washington—it starts in communities like ours. Let’s keep building from the ground up, one candidate, one conversation, one vote at a time.Thank you for all of your generous support!Becky Edwards
---
|
---

name: Team Shontel Brown
subject: Trump is trying to make it harder for people to vote – plain and simple.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Shontel Brown for Congress
body:
The SAVE Act is a propaganda bill that creates problems instead of solving them.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106029474/540188856/1806897946?refcode=EM_F_250417SAVE_N_TSB_90DO_4_NG_N_LOGO&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzg0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNDE5MWE0ZS1kNzFiLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kN7OjXpHSfbBW3HHFLH0TYa7OkdatFdgfEONa3Q_NkI=) Peter,

In case you missed it, last week, Shontel Brown voted against the SAVE Act. This radical and reckless legislation would restrict voting rights for millions of eligible American citizens and create new burdens and costs on military voters, married women, minority voters, veterans, and others. As a reminder, the SAVE Act is entirely unnecessary because _it is already illegal for non-citizens_ to vote.

The SAVE Act requires proof of citizenship to vote in federal elections. That means for most, **a driver’s license would not be enough** , and folks will need either a passport or a birth certificate to register. However, **over 140 million Americans do NOT have a passport** , and, according to the _Center for American Progress_ analysis, **over 2.6 million women in Ohio do not have a birth certificate that matches their current legal name.**

**The SAVE Act is a propaganda bill that creates problems instead of solving them. It is intended to make it harder to vote and feed Trump’s Big Lie that the 2020 election was stolen.**

[**The SAVE Act could disenfranchise millions of eligible voters. Shontel is determined to fight alongside her Democratic colleagues to protect voting rights, but she needs our help. name: Lily Tang Williams
subject: I never got to use a toilet seat
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Lily4Congress Committee
body:
I owe my life to the United States of America| | | [](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=70b02f2a7a9345219056282734938bc8&_e=B8StnvN78ZDhLQvLNbOFHtOZ0184Ec6VLXnu6ZZdDedKefxRUhFF3U-RlcjLE5mBzhysohiYqJW1B9SAg6p8osHv_fSr3NWM7e53L2y6ZHw3agyVNN_-NfhS6NfBKVglAkJR0nFCzLISvKJcEJ-EmFD4xgjUMjJ7MjyOR7PNTGg2hCT7ttwVur70TdVJbOQDjUciXLTxkunlE_Hmxr9Dic4Fm50Xh8bTavW4amElHZDbi9oT7Zh278stcgTGKaTGNKfdSF-LdPTSlGnTk-g57ubRAe6szBHFRja9NucakvlyYOgmYAdu9QoIflkbxp19doMdc06Gdsi-aDq4ryEr12CEj4c6HsOyKOz2AyVQwe8TxYanilpb_VzQf481DgKy1oOxx8dUSfM9tdjBx6KISw%3D%3D)
---
I never got to use a toilet seat. Instead, we had to share a hole in the ground with 8 other families. I was raised on food rationing coupons in the Sichuan province of China during Mao’s Cultural Revolution. I was only a child, yet I worshiped him like a God. I had no other choice. I was brainwashed. We all were. Eventually, I was able to escape from China and flee to the United States.
---
[](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=70b02f2a7a9345219056282734938bc8&_e=B8StnvN78ZDhLQvLNbOFHtOZ0184Ec6VLXnu6ZZdDedKefxRUhFF3U-RlcjLE5mBzhysohiYqJW1B9SAg6p8osHv_fSr3NWM7e53L2y6ZHw3agyVNN_-NfhS6NfBKVglAkJR0nFCzLISvKJcEJ-EmFD4xgjUMjJ7MjyOR7PNTGg2hCT7ttwVur70TdVJbOQDjUciXLTxkunlE_Hmxr9Dic4Fm50Xh8bTavW4amElHZCk1B6kPIh8_FHAOKVW-pgNE9UoBTm4GfrNx2j1Z6FQLcd9aKouqADpFoH13o3kF_i8xsT7LlKb-kZLE6fZC6eSqs_04jmdu-SCLzizlE5txVRCJxg39t_obaGGkWRGxdfyRNuPCA1Tv0iYPi8wumwbGke654D9QRaa-_ysISQdRQ%3D%3D)
---
I spent the next 20 years uninstalling everything Mao’s regime had programmed into my brain, only to discover the very same evil was now being promoted in America and celebrated by the radical left. Then it hit me. I wasn’t just a product of the American Dream - I was also a survivor of Communist rule. **[My name is Lily Tang Williams, and I’m the Republican running for Congress in New Hampshire's 2nd Congressional District.](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=70b02f2a7a9345219056282734938bc8&_e=B8StnvN78ZDhLQvLNbOFHtOZ0184Ec6VLXnu6ZZdDedKefxRUhFF3U-RlcjLE5mBzhysohiYqJW1B9SAg6p8osHv_fSr3NWM7e53L2y6ZHw3agyVNN_-NfhS6NfBKVglAkJR0nFCzLISvKJcEJ-EmFD4xgjUMjJ7MjyOR7PNTGg2hCT7ttwVur70TdVJbOQDjUciXLTxkunlE_Hmxr9Dic4Fm50Xh8bTavW4amElHZBUeIb2_CjtCZfNsN6lgPNly3CKQy53slunayCmwCSOGZlYT9BJRATB3HINV57dqv7J_Txld4lqnh49nwL7IMa3RSkC-ruer_0DoXNkxzPt1FZ-0jpPQb_SKKLViUAdMVWjIXgSxy3xCJ10trdB78nQipzy1maPi86FZr1ntScXSA%3D%3D)**
---
[**SUPPORT LILY'S CAMPAIGN**](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=70b02f2a7a9345219056282734938bc8&_e=B8StnvN78ZDhLQvLNbOFHtOZ0184Ec6VLXnu6ZZdDedKefxRUhFF3U-RlcjLE5mBzhysohiYqJW1B9SAg6p8osHv_fSr3NWM7e53L2y6ZHw3agyVNN_-NfhS6NfBKVglAkJR0nFCzLISvKJcEJ-EmFD4xgjUMjJ7MjyOR7PNTGg2hCT7ttwVur70TdVJbOQDjUciXLTxkunlE_Hmxr9Dic4Fm50Xh8bTavW4amElHZClLec0Nng0e42Os7PF6jwTdStEfLBfcyRar91zXXduJS60kmnfYKAC0HJhVuF8dNx-JaDuWxQYyJ2ZL5MTIpG1NmdZiQeIaBFo8lpFJvNNEC-k4Pw6LqOpGDvrJqq-KDPv-j1sBqXM7owwEzNW_HjVnoc96q3Z4REUve0HiUvFJw%3D%3D)
---
My story, my voice, and my campaign endanger everything the Democrats have built their party on - _I don’t fit their mold_. **[And now they’re preparing to spend millions to browbeat me into silence& end my candidacy.](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=70b02f2a7a9345219056282734938bc8&_e=B8StnvN78ZDhLQvLNbOFHtOZ0184Ec6VLXnu6ZZdDedKefxRUhFF3U-RlcjLE5mBzhysohiYqJW1B9SAg6p8osHv_fSr3NWM7e53L2y6ZHw3agyVNN_-NfhS6NfBKVglAkJR0nFCzLISvKJcEJ-EmFD4xgjUMjJ7MjyOR7PNTGg2hCT7ttwVur70TdVJbOQDjUciXLTxkunlE_Hmxr9Dic4Fm50Xh8bTavW4amElHZC0D7gjJNJhUz0uec4yK5_L0Frxrjtoda22IRnreElBg2c0Tn7xGUV2Qe3g-8vN6a6djVXeSTqKG7mvfVOk9P36cOFzJSS0d8ut46um-MtbM_akDpEdx90TXVItURK5zWyg4cWrfAOuOrEx8YZtj34dD8AnObB55QREi_RrbsTrmA%3D%3D)** That’s why I’m humbly asking for your help today. My candidacy & our country truly do depend on it. I hope you’ll do what you can to save both today. Help me make history, Patriot.

clean_body: I owe my life to the United States of America| | | I never got to use a toilet seat. Instead, we had to share a hole in the ground with 8 other families. I was raised on food rationing coupons in the Sichuan province of China during Mao’s Cultural Revolution. I was only a child, yet I worshiped him like a God. I had no other choice. I was brainwashed. We all were. Eventually, I was able to escape from China and flee to the United States. I spent the next 20 years uninstalling everything Mao’s regime had programmed into my brain, only to discover the very same evil was now being promoted in America and celebrated by the radical left. Then it hit me. I wasn’t just a product of the American Dream - I was also a survivor of Communist rule. My story, my voice, and my campaign endanger everything the Democrats have built their party on - I don’t fit their mold. That’s why I’m humbly asking for your help today. My candidacy & our country truly do depend on it. I hope you’ll do what you can to save both today. Help me make history, Patriot. God Bless America,Lily Tang Williams | | | | Paid for by Lily4Congress Committee | |

In a moment, I’m going to tell you about my vision for this election — and I would love it if you took the time to read my full message. But if you’re short on time, will you split an $10 contribution to my campaign and Ohio House Dems to become one of my founding donors and help me become the next Democratic U.S. Senator? >>**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106029664/540189566/-701326135?refcode=250417_Stevenson_Mitch&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDI2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjOGM0MGQyNC1kODFiLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kGIrf94sZVR_b-mYG4NaOx3CuF8cD0Bu-ftuOvuxOf8=) Friend, I was born and raised right here in Kentucky. I’ve spent my life in service to our communities, state, and country. I served as a JAG attorney in the U.S. Air Force for 27 years with the rank of Colonel. In the Air Force, I successfully negotiated several hostage crises and prosecuted violent criminals and child sex offenders. I'm a nonprofit founder for legal services veterans and seniors in need, ordained minister, and the Floor Leader of the Kentucky House Democrats. I know firsthand the challenges Kentucky and our country are up against. [**So now, after 27 years of serving our state and our country, I am running for Senate, but I can't start this fight alone. That’s why I’m reaching out to you personally: Will you split a $10 donation between my campaign and Ohio House Dems before my deadline at midnight TONIGHT to help me kick off my campaign strong and show the world I have what it takes to win this race? >>**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106029665/540189567/-701326135?refcode=250417_Stevenson_Mitch&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDI2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjOGM0MGQyNC1kODFiLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kGIrf94sZVR_b-mYG4NaOx3CuF8cD0Bu-ftuOvuxOf8=) I’m ready to run for Senate to defend our freedoms and build an economy that works for everyone – no matter your background. Every American deserves access to food, clean water, healthcare, education, and shelter. **But Mitch McConnell and the GOP do not see things the same way.** During his 40 years in the Senate, he’s put his personal quest for power and his special-interest allies ahead of our state, nation, and Constitution every time. I’m ready to turn the page. But I need you with me. [**I promise you I will put everything I have into winning this race and being a voice for every single American. But I can’t do it alone — and I’ll need the help of grassroots supporters like you every step of the way. So I’m humbly asking: Will you split a $10 between my campaign and Ohio House Dems before my deadline at midnight tonight and help me kick off my campaign with an unbeatable show of support? >>**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106029666/540189568/-701326135?refcode=250417_Stevenson_Mitch&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDI2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjOGM0MGQyNC1kODFiLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kGIrf94sZVR_b-mYG4NaOx3CuF8cD0Bu-ftuOvuxOf8=) | | [**$10 »**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106029667/540189569/-791995096?amount=10&refcode=250417_Stevenson_Mitch&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDI2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjOGM0MGQyNC1kODFiLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kGIrf94sZVR_b-mYG4NaOx3CuF8cD0Bu-ftuOvuxOf8= "https://secure.actblue.com/")

Franklin Graham delivered the prayer service at the White House Easter prayer service and dinner. President Trump declared "It will always be In God We Trust. We will never change that... With God's help, we can overcome every challenge, triumph over every evil, and restore the spirit of faith in the United States." Restoring the spirit of faith in America is a stark contrast from Biden's proclamation of Transgender Visibility day on Easter just last year. The Left is once again wrapped up in their obsession of celebrating bad guys, this time around its Kilmar Abrego Garcia. Senator Chris Van Hollen failed to secure a meeting with deported immigrant Kilmar Abrego Garcia in his attempt to bring Garcia "home." AOC and Cory Booker are singing the same tune but unfortunately for them, Abrego is home, as he is a citizen of El Salvador. Even more, the Prince George county gang unit identified him as a gang member back in 2019. The Trump administration obliterated the Senator from Maryland when they brought in angel mom Patty Morin to share how Senator Van Hollen never even acknowledged her daughter, Rachel Morin, when she was murdered by an illegal immigrant. The IRS is looking to strip Harvard of its tax-exempt status because Harvard is choosing to be a nesting ground for anti-semitism and woke-radical lunatics. Letitia James may face jail time for mortgage fraud of all things, her vengeful campaign against President Trump included his real estate dealings, while she herself was guilty the entire. The White house drops a checkmate on the Associated Press... PLUS this week's winners and losers!

---
Do you recognize the young girl in the photo above? Back then it was her bangs, favorite pair of overalls, and big dreams against the world!
While I’m not rocking the bangs or overalls much these days, those dreams that formed during my childhood years never died.
I've always known I wanted to spend my life fighting for the state I love and the people that make it great. That's why I chose a career where I’m able to make a difference in the lives of Michiganders.
**And if I had to do it all over again, I wouldn’t change a thing.**
Because Michiganders put their faith in me for the past three terms, we’ve shaped this district for the better by:
* Fixing the chips shortage (using the CHIPS Act that Elon Musk is trying to destroy)
* Increasing jobs
* Investing in infrastructure projects across Oakland County
* Defending reproductive rights for all Michigan women
* And more.

Our progress, and the people who are trying to reverse it, are the reason I wake up every morning ready to keep doing what I’m doing.
Young Haley would be so proud of all we’ve accomplished together and all that we’re fighting for.
[**We can’t let a couple of bad actors get in the way of the work we’ve done. If you want to stay in the know about how I’m fighting back to protect our communities, you can follow me on socials!**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106030174/540191288/437933856?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODA3NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNjIzMTA2Ny1kOTFiLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=AjYrbr8HX6J-YDV8yoi6CI-3u7QQ_qzNU88xq4tVJzU=&emci=64f8f231-021b-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=c6231067-d91b-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=5999589)
